Add blog: Visualizing Dense vs MoE IB communication at 10ms resolution

- MoE: continuous but volatile traffic (CV=0.42) from competing All-to-All and FSDP collectives
- Network design implications: latency, throughput, congestion, scheduling

ci: fix release pipeline — merge version-bump into release workflow (#1)

* ci: merge version-bump into release workflow

Fix critical bug: version-bump.yml created tags using GITHUB_TOKEN,
but events from GITHUB_TOKEN don't trigger other workflows, so
release.yml would never fire.

Now a single release workflow handles everything on push to master:
1. Determine version bump type (conventional commits)
2. Compute next semver version
3. Build, sign, and publish to Maven Central + GitHub Packages
4. Commit version bump + create tag
5. Create GitHub Release

CI workflow now only runs on PRs (validation before merge).

commit.message:
feat: record a status report

I added Tuist as a development tool. Tuist allows me to build the Apple
application configuration using Swift and then generate Xcode projects
and the workspace from that configuration. This is a much more
maintainable approach than manually maintaining Xcode project files, and
it also allows me to generate the Xcode project files on demand, which
is helpful for keeping the project files clean and avoiding merge
conflicts.

I updated the software requirements document to include mise-en-place,
Xcodes.app, and Xcode.

I created RecordingSession, an NSObject subclass conforming to
AVCaptureFileOutputRecordingDelegate, which manages AVCaptureSession
lifecycle on a dedicated serial DispatchQueue and handles partial
recording preservation via AVErrorRecordingSuccessfullyFinishedKey.

I created CameraPreview, a UIViewRepresentable that hosts a UIView
whose layerClass is AVCaptureVideoPreviewLayer, providing an
efficient GPU-composited camera preview for SwiftUI.

I created RecordingViewModel, an @Observable @MainActor class that
manages recording state transitions, a countdown timer using Swift
structured concurrency, permission checking with AVCaptureDevice,
and VoiceOver announcements at the 10s and 5s thresholds.

I created CountdownTimerView, a circular progress gauge that
colour-codes urgency (yellow at 10s, red at 5s) with a companion
text label for WCAG 1.4.1 compliance.

I created RecordingViewModelTests and CountdownTimerTests using
Swift Testing to cover state transitions, timer countdown,
urgency boundary transitions, re-record, and early stop.

I created RecordingScreen, which composes the camera preview,
countdown timer overlay, record/stop controls, permission denied
guidance, and an error banner. It navigates to ReviewScreen
when recording completes.

I created VideoPlayerView, a thin AVKit VideoPlayer wrapper that
creates an AVPlayer from a local file URL.

I created ReviewScreen, which plays back the recorded video and
provides a Re-record button with a discard confirmation alert.
Navigating back via the iOS back gesture also cleans up the
finished recording.

I updated MainView to add a NavigationStack with a list entry
linking to RecordingScreen.

I created the architecture decision record documenting the choice
to use AVFoundation directly over UIImagePickerController, the
RecordingSessionProtocol abstraction, the concurrency model using
nonisolated(unsafe) with a dedicated session DispatchQueue, partial
recording preservation via AVErrorRecordingSuccessfullyFinishedKey,
the UIViewRepresentable camera preview bridge, and the @Observable
@MainActor ViewModel pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement authentication and authorisation backend (Identity + JWT) (#130)

* Initial plan

* feat: implement authentication backend with Identity models and Cosmos stores

- Add BlendUser, BlendRole, ExternalLoginInfo domain models in Blend.Domain/Identity
- Add CosmosUserStore implementing IUserStore, IUserPasswordStore, IUserEmailStore,
  IUserLoginStore, IUserRoleStore backed by IRepository<BlendUser>
- Add CosmosRoleStore implementing IRoleStore with in-memory predefined roles
- Register IRepository<BlendUser> in CosmosServiceExtensions for the 'users' container
- Add Microsoft.Extensions.Identity.Core 9.0.3 package to Blend.Infrastructure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement complete authentication API layer

- Add RefreshToken domain entity (Blend.Domain)
- Add JwtOptions configuration class
- Add IJwtService/JwtService for JWT access token generation
- Add IEmailService/ConsoleEmailService for password reset emails
- Add IRefreshTokenService/InMemoryRefreshTokenService for refresh tokens
- Add auth DTOs: RegisterRequest, LoginRequest, ForgotPasswordRequest,
  ResetPasswordRequest, AuthResponse
- Add AuthServiceExtensions with AddBlendAuthentication():
  - ASP.NET Core Identity with CosmosUserStore/CosmosRoleStore (no EF)
  - JWT Bearer authentication with configurable options
  - Conditional Google, Facebook, Twitter/X OAuth providers
  - Authorization policies: RequireAuthenticated, RequireAdmin
  - Rate limiting: fixed window 10 req/min per IP on auth endpoints
- Add AuthController with endpoints:
  POST /api/v1/auth/register
  POST /api/v1/auth/login
  POST /api/v1/auth/login/{provider} (external OAuth)
  GET  /api/v1/auth/callback/{provider}
  POST /api/v1/auth/refresh (HttpOnly cookie-based)
  POST /api/v1/auth/logout
  POST /api/v1/auth/forgot-password
  POST /api/v1/auth/reset-password
- Update Program.cs: replace placeholder auth with AddBlendAuthentication()
  and add UseRateLimiter() middleware
- Update appsettings.json: expand Jwt section, add Authentication section
  for Google/Facebook/Twitter credentials

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix code review issues: use DateTimeOffset.UtcNow in JwtService and add null guard for redirectUri in ExternalLogin

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: safe token logging + Twitter OAuth claim mapping

- ConsoleEmailService: log email at Info level, token at Debug only to
  prevent accidental exposure in production log aggregators
- AuthServiceExtensions: add full Twitter OnCreatingTicket handler that
  fetches user info and maps id/name/email claims from Twitter v2 API
  response ({ data: { id, name, email } })

- RefreshTokenServiceTests: InMemoryRefreshTokenService create/get/revoke/expiry

fix: sprint intervals never parsed from Intervals.icu API

Root cause: /intervals endpoint returns { icu_intervals: [...] } (an object),
not a bare array. The code checked Array.isArray(raw) which always failed,
causing every activity to silently return 0 intervals — hence 'No interval
history — using Vmax model only' on every session.

Three fixes applied:

1. useIntervalsData.ts — read raw.icu_intervals instead of raw
   (falls back to raw itself if the API ever returns a bare array)

2. parser.ts — filter rest periods that Intervals.icu labels WORK
   Intervals.icu sometimes records the walk-back recovery between sprint
   reps as a WORK segment (e.g. 300 s, 63 m, avg 0.21 m/s). These have a
   high max_speed from the preceding sprint but very low average_speed.
   New MIN_SPRINT_AVG_SPEED = 4.0 m/s check rejects them.

3. parser.ts + schema.ts — accept RECOVERY/INTERVAL/LAP interval types
   Switched from an allowlist (WORK, ACTIVE only) to a denylist
   (REST, ACTIVE_REST, WARMUP, COOLDOWN, RECOVERY). This lets auto-detected
   efforts (type INTERVAL or LAP) through to the distance/speed filter.

4. schema.ts — add training_load field
   The actual API field is training_load not icu_training_load.

Tests: 6 new parser tests added (RECOVERY exclusion, INTERVAL/LAP inclusion,
low-average-speed WORK rejection, no-average_speed pass-through).
All 18 test files pass (459 tests total).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(fsutil): replace strings.HasPrefix with filepath.Rel in ReadFileSafe (#3266)

* fix(fsutil): replace strings.HasPrefix with filepath.Rel in ReadFileSafe

The previous strings.HasPrefix check did not enforce directory
boundaries: a sibling path like "/base_evil/file" would pass the
prefix test for base "/base" because the string "/base_evil/file"
does start with the string "/base".

Replace the check with filepath.Rel so that only paths whose relative
form does NOT begin with ".." are allowed. Also clean basePath with
filepath.Clean (only filePath was cleaned before) so that callers
passing un-cleaned base paths are handled correctly.

Add a regression test case ("prefix attack - sibling directory")
that exercises this exact vulnerability.

commit.message:
feat: add 12-week periodized sprint training plan

Implements a full 12-week training plan for 100m–400m sprint development,
anchored to upcoming race events from Intervals.icu. The plan auto-detects
which week the athlete is in and generates an NFI-adjusted workout for today.

Phases:
- Weeks 1–4: GPP — acceleration mechanics, tempo conditioning
- Weeks 5–8: SPP — speed endurance, special endurance (200–300 m)
- Weeks 9–10: Pre-Competition — race-specific speed, sharpening
- Weeks 11–12: Competition — taper, volume reduction, race prep

New files:
- src/domain/sprint/training-plan.ts — 12-week plan spec + workout generators
- src/components/TrainingPlanPanel.tsx — UI: week progress, today's session
- tests/domain/sprint/training-plan.test.ts — 29 tests (all passing)

Modified files:
- src/hooks/useIntervalsData.ts — adds trainingPlan to IntervalsDataState
- src/components/Dashboard.tsx — renders TrainingPlanPanel when plan active
- src/App.tsx — passes trainingPlan prop to Dashboard

NFI integration:
- Green NFI → full prescribed session
- Amber NFI → volume reduced ~25 %
- Red + negative TSB (fatigued) → converted to recovery session
- Red + positive TSB (stale) → kept with re-activation note

All 454 tests passing. TypeScript clean.

feat: server-side OAuth token exchange proxy via Vercel

Intervals.icu requires client_secret on the server side. Instead of
embedding it in the browser bundle, add a Vercel serverless function at

- vercel.json: route /api/oauth/token before the catch-all /api/(.*) rule

Setup required:
  Vercel env vars: OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET, OAUTH_ALLOWED_ORIGIN
  GitHub secret:   VITE_OAUTH_PROXY_URL=https://<vercel-url>/api/oauth/token

commit.message:
fix: code review findings and tmux status-bar flash (#30)

When toggling Aiwaku while a tmux session already existed, set-option
status off ran after the terminal had already rendered, causing a brief
status-bar flash (race condition, see #29).

Fix: pass -d to new-session so the session is created detached; apply
set-option status off; then attach-session to connect the terminal.
The status bar is hidden before any rendering occurs.

Three issues found during code review:

1. Operator-precedence bug in send.lua format_diagnostic():
   d.source and ("[" .. d.source or "unknown" .. "] ") or ""
   produced "[lua_ls" instead of "[lua_ls] " because .. binds tighter
   than or. Fixed to: d.source and ("[" .. d.source .. "] ") or ""

2. Cross-tab window guard in session.restore_session():
   vim.fn.win_findbuf() returns windows from all tabpages. If the ghost
   buffer window was in a different tab, state.win_id would be set to a
   cross-tab window, violating the invariant that the sidebar must always
   be in the current tabpage. Now guards with
   window.win_visible_in_current_tab(ghost_win), consistent with the
   pattern used throughout the rest of the codebase.

3. Doc mismatch: toggle() LuaDoc comment and doc/aiwaku.nvim.txt both
   omitted the CWD-based session reconnect logic. Both updated to describe
   the three-step fallback: resume -> reconnect by CWD -> create new.

Add verification section and expand code review checklist

lua.instructions.md: add 'Verification after changes' section with the
required load-time smoke check command, diff review steps, public API
surface list, optional-dependency safety note, and guidance for when no
automated tests exist.

copilot-instructions.md: expand Code Review section with explicit
Validation steps (smoke check + git diff --check + end-to-end API trace)
and a Checklist covering: no errors, no regressions, docs match
implementation, no redundancies, no performance issues, and unified
patterns (don't introduce a second way to do the same thing).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
